title: [CI:DOCS] Multi-arch image workflow: Make steps generic
body:
#### What type of PR is this?
/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Since this workflow is duplicated across three repositories, maintaining
changes becomes onerous if the item contents vary between
implementations in any way.  Improve this situation by encoding the
repository-specific details into env. vars. then referencing those vars
throughout.  This way, a meaningful diff can be worked with to compare
the contents across repositories.

Also included are abstractions for the specific command used to obtain
the project version, and needed details for filtering the output.  Both
of these vary across the Buildah, Skopeo, and Podman repos.

***NOTE:*** This change requires the names of two github action secrets
to be updated: `BUILDAH_QUAY_USERNAME` -> `REPONAME_QUAY_USERNAME`
(and `*PASSWORD`).

#### How to verify it

Merge the PR and if it fails, fix it in a new PR :sob:
(this is github's fault)

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

* Github-actions workflow is very head-hurtie to review / understand.  I apologize in advance :disappointed: 
* For the most part, this simply replaces repository-specific bits, with variables, for example `buildah` -> `$REPONAME`.
* Hard-coding the `$REPONAME` value is a compromise.  In all it's wisdom, guthub chose to **not** provide this value in any default environment variables :angry: They only give it in the form of `<user>/<repo>'.  It would be an entire additional workflow step to parse out `<repo>` to provide it as an env. var.  i.e. it's easier to just hard-code it :disappointed: 
* There (really) is no good way to test these changes, they must run from the main branch in order to enable access to the secrets :disappointed: 

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

None

comments.body: @edsantiago PTAL when you get a moment.  I've stared at the changes as carefully as I can, but could use your more discerning eyes.  My intention after this merges, and is proven functional, is to open similar/identical PRs in the other repos.  My hope is, (besides the `env` section), it can be a literal copy-paste of the workflow.

LGTM
but I'm not a subject expert by any means.

@TomSweeneyRedHat nobody is...not even the github developers who write it :smirk: 

Seriously though, this PR should has very little in the way of functionality changes (only `$VERSION_CMD` and related), it's mostly just renaming stuff.
